speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: So, lie thou there. Die thou, and die our fear,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: For Warwick was a bug that feared us all.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Now, Montague, sit fast. I seek for thee,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: That Warwick’s bones may keep thine company.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Ah, who is nigh? Come to me, friend or foe,

speaker: WARWICK
text: And tell me who is victor, York or Warwick?

speaker: WARWICK
text: Why ask I that? My mangled body shows,

speaker: WARWICK
text: My blood, my want of strength, my sick heart shows

speaker: WARWICK
text: That I must yield my body to the earth

speaker: WARWICK
text: And, by my fall, the conquest to my foe.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Thus yields the cedar to the axe’s edge,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Whose arms gave shelter to the princely eagle,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Under whose shade the ramping lion slept,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Whose top branch overpeered Jove’s spreading tree

speaker: WARWICK
text: And kept low shrubs from winter’s pow’rful wind.

speaker: WARWICK
text: These eyes, that now are dimmed with death’s black veil,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Have been as piercing as the midday sun

speaker: WARWICK
text: To search the secret treasons of the world.

speaker: WARWICK
text: The wrinkles in my brows, now filled with blood,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Were likened oft to kingly sepulchers,

speaker: WARWICK
text: For who lived king but I could dig his grave?

speaker: WARWICK
text: And who durst smile when Warwick bent his brow?

speaker: WARWICK
text: Lo, now my glory smeared in dust and blood!

speaker: WARWICK
text: My parks, my walks, my manors that I had

speaker: WARWICK
text: Even now forsake me; and of all my lands

speaker: WARWICK
text: Is nothing left me but my body’s length.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Why, what is pomp, rule, reign, but earth and dust?

speaker: WARWICK
text: And live we how we can, yet die we must.

speaker: SOMERSET
text: Ah, Warwick, Warwick, wert thou as we are,

speaker: SOMERSET
text: We might recover all our loss again.

speaker: SOMERSET
text: The Queen from France hath brought a puissant power;

speaker: SOMERSET
text: Even now we heard the news. Ah, could’st thou fly—

speaker: WARWICK
text: Why, then, I would not fly. Ah, Montague,

speaker: WARWICK
text: If thou be there, sweet brother, take my hand

speaker: WARWICK
text: And with thy lips keep in my soul awhile.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Thou lov’st me not, for, brother, if thou didst,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Thy tears would wash this cold congealèd blood

speaker: WARWICK
text: That glues my lips and will not let me speak.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Come quickly, Montague, or I am dead.

speaker: SOMERSET
text: Ah, Warwick, Montague hath breathed his last,

speaker: SOMERSET
text: And to the latest gasp cried out for Warwick,

speaker: SOMERSET
text: And said “Commend me to my valiant brother.”

speaker: SOMERSET
text: And more he would have said, and more he spoke,

speaker: SOMERSET
text: Which sounded like a cannon in a vault,

speaker: SOMERSET
text: That mought not be distinguished, but at last

speaker: SOMERSET
text: I well might hear, delivered with a groan,

speaker: SOMERSET
text: “O, farewell, Warwick.”

speaker: WARWICK
text: Sweet rest his soul! Fly, lords, and save yourselves,

speaker: WARWICK
text: For Warwick bids you all farewell to meet in heaven.

speaker: OXFORD
text: Away, away, to meet the Queen’s great power!
